pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 58: title = "Hypertension Study - Subject Screening (Protocol H-2024)"

Regex pattern verification: correctly matches format 'XXX-000' (3 uppercase letters, hyphen, 3 digits). Tested against examples: ABC-123 (match), abc-123 (no match), ABCD-123 (no match). Task description line 1 confirms this format requirement.
task.json description and task.sh both reference exact tip text. Task description: "Add a validation tip: 'Format must be XXX-000'."

task.json and task description specify Minimum: 70 for Systolic BP. Medical reasonableness verified: normal systolic <120, hypotension threshold ~90, minimum of 70 is reasonable. Reference: American Heart Association blood pressure guidelines.

task.json and task description specify Maximum: 220 for Systolic BP. Medical reasonableness verified: hypertensive crisis is >180 mmHg, 220 is above crisis threshold, appropriate for hypertension research study. Reference: American Heart Association blood pressure guidelines.
task.json and task description specify Minimum: 40 for Diastolic BP. Reasonable lower bound for medical data.

task.json and task description specify Maximum: 120 for Diastolic BP. Medical reasonableness verified: 120 diastolic is the hypertensive crisis threshold (reading >180/120 is crisis). Reference: American Heart Association blood pressure guidelines.

Task description specifies validation equation: 'ensure the date cannot be in the future (e.g., using strtotime comparisons against 'now')'. The metadata keywords directly correspond to these requirements. Verifier checks for all three keywords present in the equation.

privileged_info_summary: The Data Integrity Hardening task for LimeSurvey uses a synthetic survey titled 'Hypertension Study - Subject Screening (Protocol H-2024)' that is created fresh during task setup via the LimeSurvey JSON-RPC API. The survey contains four validated input fields: (1) Subject ID requires regex pattern ^[A-Z]{3}-[0-9]{3}$ for format XXX-000 with validation tip 'Format must be XXX-000'; (2) Systolic Blood Pressure accepts numeric values between 70-220 mmHg; (3) Diastolic Blood Pressure accepts numeric values between 40-120 mmHg; (4) Date of Screening requires validation equation using strtotime function with <= operator comparing against 'now' to prevent future dates. All metadata values are consistent with task requirements and medically reasonable for a hypertension research study.
